channel.title: Is it possible to use Materialize with Emotion styled components
channel.description:
Hi again :grinning:
Do you know, is it possible to use Materialize classes with Emotion components? I start to look at that on this week, but I thought if you know the answer already. It would be nice to use both.

item.title: Is it possible to use Materialize with Emotion styled components
item.dc:creator: luisherranz
item.description:
marika.kononen:

I will let you know the results of my testing

Please do!

Read full topic
item.source: Is it possible to use Materialize with Emotion styled components

item.title: Is it possible to use Materialize with Emotion styled components
item.dc:creator: marika.kononen
item.description:
Ok, thanks for this info and I will let you know the results of my testing.

Read full topic
item.source: Is it possible to use Materialize with Emotion styled components

item.title: Is it possible to use Materialize with Emotion styled components
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
Hi!

I’m not sure how those would interact together, mostly because Materialize CSS has its own JS, and that might interfere with your expectations on how React components should behave. I’d go for something like Material UI that supports styled components.

They explain how to style their components here. The example uses styled-components but I think it should work the same with emotion, so you’d just use import { styled } from 'frontity'.

If you give that a try, let us know if it works!

Read full topic
item.source: Is it possible to use Materialize with Emotion styled components

I start to look at that on this week, but I thought if you know the answer already. It would be nice to use both.

Read full topic
